findings.detail: No minimum-pool-size constant exists in code; the 'above minimum pool' invariant has no threshold to check against. Daily uses a W×30 horizon (scripts/daily-pool-audit). OPEN QUESTION for Eric: define the min tier pool size.
fil voice = fil-PH-Wavenet-A (locale fil-PH); configured in 1 place(s)

184 unique words emitted to audio-manifest.txt. Cache coverage lives on the server (audio_cache, keyed md5('{lang}:'+word)); not verifiable from the repo and NOT probed (probing /api/speak would bulk-generate). Verify against the cache in a separate approved run.

Automated homophone detection is Spanish-only. Filipino has real same-sound/different-spelling homophones that a spell-by-ear player could miss, but flagging them needs a native speaker + phonetic transcription. AUDITOR TASK: list any list words whose pronunciation matches another common word, so grading can accept-any (the accept-any mechanism in src/homophones.rs already supports any language via assets/words/fil/homophones.txt).

fil seed layer: 92 terms. Curation scan below is language-scoped (fil seed + universal hard slurs). Runtime My Words screening (src/profanity.rs is_blocked) separately uses the 1768-term all-language union — that over-block is intentional for user imports.
'aso' is a valid fil word but is on the profanity seed for: ['nl']. Not flagged for fil (kept per decision addendum). Note: it stays blocked in free-text usernames via the global/English path.
'guro' is a valid fil word but is on the profanity seed for: ['en']. Not flagged for fil (kept per decision addendum). Note: it stays blocked in free-text usernames via the global/English path.
'pinto' is a valid fil word but is on the profanity seed for: ['pt']. Not flagged for fil (kept per decision addendum). Note: it stays blocked in free-text usernames via the global/English path.
